slides.action_title: DeepSeek Sparse Attention cuts cost ==O(L²)→O(Lk)== with no quality loss
points: A lightning indexer keeps the ==k|top-k== tokens per query (==k = 2048==), so attention scales ==k|linearly== in context length.
==128K== context is retained; DSA is the ==k|only== architectural change from V3.1-Terminus.
==p|Matches or beats== V3.1 on MMLU-Pro, GPQA and AIME while cutting inference cost sharply.
The Speciale variant took ==p|gold== at IMO, IOI, ICPC and CMO 2025.
slides.speaker_notes: The one idea: sparse attention via a learned top-k selector, linear in context, no quality regression.

slides.action_title: Head-to-head: an open model within striking distance, winning some

table.caption: Selected benchmarks — DeepSeek-V3.2-Thinking vs leading closed models (row-best in bold)

slides.speaker_notes: Not the top line everywhere, but it takes SWE Multilingual — a real win for an open model.

slides.action_title: Strengths granted — here is what I would push on

points.head: “Surpasses GPT-5” is the ==w|expensive variant==
points.body: The frontier claim is Speciale, whose RL alone exceeds ==10%== of pre-training compute. The shipped V3.2-Thinking is ==w|competitive but not ahead==. (my analysis)

points.head: Fixed ==k = 2048== selected tokens
points.body: Selection is ==w|capped regardless of L==; tasks needing broad long-range recall could lose what the indexer down-ranks. (my analysis)

slides.speaker_notes: Two honest pushes: the frontier framing conflates variants, and the fixed k could cap long-range recall.

questions: Does fixed ==k = 2048== hurt tasks needing ==k|broad== long-range recall?
How much of the frontier claim rides on the ==w|expensive Speciale variant==?
Would DSA transfer to ==k|smaller open models== on commodity GPUs?
slides.speaker_notes: Open the floor: cost, generality, and how much rides on the expensive variant.
